topic: Bermuda/Japan symmetry — c7→c8 verification of H1/H2/H4 patches
change_summary: Decider commit 417a887c (c7→c8) applied three named patches: finding rewritten quantitative-first, tldr_evidence rewritten with kernel acknowledgement, detail_evidence steelman heading tightened. A subsequent workspace-sync commit (7b6eef39) additionally closed the c7 minor carry-overs H5/H6/H7 via FUSE-side operator edits (Devil's Sea anchored to Berlitz 1974, vague-reading paragraph replaced 50° heuristic with pure-unfalsifiability framing, WMM 2025 URL anchored).
our_argument_summary: Bermuda/Japan claim refuted on three independent grounds: (1) 28° longitude gap is 15.6% relative miss, ~3× the dome's own WIN-029 5.3% confirmation benchmark; (2) without published tolerance the claim is unfalsifiable; (3) NOAA/Lloyd's/USCG all confirm no anomalous loss rates. Charitable steelman of agonic-line correlation also fails — neither agonic line passes through either folklore region.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No monitor.py entry for WIN-028. No script processes this claim. The dome simply asserts the longitude symmetry on the predictions page text.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: N/A — no validation code exists for this WIN.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: N/A — no published tolerance or rejection rule.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None. Detail_verdict_text correctly states "No monitor.py entry exists for this WIN."

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: monitor.py contains no Bermuda/Japan symmetry check, no longitude calculation, no folklore-zone coordinate processing. The claim is text-only on the dome's predictions page.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: No standard-physics phenomenon predicts paired folklore zones at near-antipodal longitudes. There is nothing to relabel.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: The Bermuda Triangle (popularised 1950s) and Devil's Sea (Berlitz 1974) are pre-existing folklore zones with coordinates established on nautical charts decades before the dome model. The dome adopted these locations and declared them confirmatory without publishing a prior derivation from dome geometry.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No derivation from disc_radius / firmament_height / aetheric parameters to the claimed longitude separation. The claim is purely an observation about two pre-existing folklore-zone coordinates.

patch_verification.queue_reason: Decider patched WIN-028: tldr_evidence (kernel acknowledgement), finding (quantitative-first), steelman heading (c7 H1/H2/H4). Verify patches landed cleanly.

h1_tldr_evidence.target: Rewrite tldr_evidence to acknowledge the kernel while making the verdict clear
h1_tldr_evidence.current_text_excerpt: The longitudes of the Bermuda Triangle and Devil's Sea really do sit near opposite sides of the globe — but 28° (15.6%) short of true antipodal...
h1_tldr_evidence.assessment: PASS with one minor stylistic concern (see holes_found H1). Kernel acknowledgement is now first; quantitative miss is concrete; NOAA/Lloyd's/USCG triad is intact; final sentence covers mechanism gap. Decider added "(~5%)" parenthetical not in c7 recommendation — minor enrichment, not a defect.

h2_finding.target: Rewrite finding to lead with the 28° quantitative argument
h2_finding.current_text: 28° gap from antipodal symmetry (15.6%, ~3× dome's 5.3% threshold); no anomalous loss rates per NOAA and Lloyd's
h2_finding.assessment: PASS. Quantitative leg now leads the summary row; disappearance-refutation leg preserved as secondary clause. Reading-order matches the verdict reasoning chain in detail_evidence.

h4_steelman_heading.target: Tighten heading to make steelman framing leading rather than parenthetical
h4_steelman_heading.current_heading: Steelman: the strongest available mechanism (none published by the dome).
h4_steelman_heading.assessment: PASS. Exact match for c7-H4 recommendation. The heading now explicitly attributes the mechanism-supplying role to us, not the dome.

bonus_h5_h6_h7.target: c7 minor carry-overs from c6 (Devil's Sea latitude anchoring, 50° vague-tolerance, WMM 2025 URL)
bonus_h5_h6_h7.source_commit: Workspace-sync 7b6eef39 (FUSE-side operator edits applied 2026-05-12T05:23-05:45 window before 06:05 sync)
bonus_h5_h6_h7.assessment: PASS for all three. Devil's Sea now anchored to Berlitz 1974 with explicit 25–35°N folklore band note; vague-reading paragraph replaced the suspicious 50° heuristic with the stronger pure-unfalsifiability framing ("any pair within that unstated band passes"); WMM 2025 URL hyperlinked to ncei.noaa.gov.

holes_found.description: tldr_evidence attributes a numeric threshold to the dome that the dome does not explicitly publish. The phrase "well outside the dome's own confirmation thresholds (~5%)" implies the dome has a published 5%-ish confirmation threshold. In fact the detail_evidence explicitly disclaims this: "we do not endorse 5.3% as a meaningful tolerance... cite it here as an internal-consistency benchmark only." The TLDR drops the caveat and presents "~5%" as if it were the dome's published threshold. A defender lands the hit: "Your TLDR invents a 5% threshold we never adopted, then uses it to refute our claim." The detail saves the argument by caveating, but the TLDR is what most readers see.
holes_found.recommendation: Soften the TLDR's framing. Suggested: "well outside the single-digit residuals the dome treats as confirmatory at WIN-029" — or just drop "(~5%)" entirely since the immediately following sentences carry the load. The plural "thresholds" is slightly more defensible than singular "threshold (~5%)" but still implies multiple published thresholds the dome does not in fact publish.

holes_found.description: Finding field "~3× dome's 5.3% threshold" carries the same threshold-attribution issue as TLDR but in the summary table where readers cannot easily expand for caveats. The detail caveats the 5.3% as "internal-consistency benchmark only — not as an independently valid standard"; the summary row doesn't. A defender attacking the table view will say the verdict rests on a fabricated dome threshold.
holes_found.recommendation: Tighten to "~3× dome's 5.3% WIN-029 benchmark" (28 chars added — fits) or "~3× dome's WIN-029 confirmation residual." Either makes clear we are citing one specific dome example rather than a published rule. The current phrasing is acceptable as shorthand but a tighter variant would close the defender attack surface cleanly.

holes_found.description: c7-H1 recommendation explicitly called for the TLDR to cover three things: quantitative miss + disappearance refutation + mechanism + tolerance gap. The decider's tldr_evidence covers the first three but drops "tolerance" from the final sentence ("The dome publishes no mechanism for the symmetry claim" vs recommended "the dome publishes no tolerance or mechanism that would convert this near-coincidence into a CONFIRMED prediction"). The tolerance gap IS implicit in the first sentence's "outside the dome's own confirmation thresholds," so this is a soft drop not a hard one; but a defender could pin the TLDR to the weaker mechanism-only complaint.
holes_found.recommendation: Either append " or tolerance" to the final sentence ("The dome publishes no mechanism or tolerance for the symmetry claim.") or rephrase to match the c7-H1 recommendation more closely. Low-priority polish.

kernel_of_truth.description: The longitudes of the Bermuda Triangle (~71°W) and the Devil's Sea (~137°E) really do sit near opposite sides of the globe (wins.json:WIN-028.detail_evidence kernel paragraph). The 152° (or 208° conjugate) separation is a real geographic observation, not a fabrication; the dome did not invent the coordinates. Folklore zones at roughly antipodal longitudes is a noticeable coincidence at the level of casual map-inspection, and the dome flagging it as worth examining is not unreasonable on its face.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The kernel collapses under two independent tests. (1) Quantitative: |152−180|=28° is a 15.6% miss relative to true antipodal symmetry (wins.json:WIN-028.finding), well outside the single-digit residuals the dome treats as confirmatory elsewhere in V51.1 (e.g., wins.json:WIN-029 5.3% Schumann residual). (2) Unfalsifiability: the dome publishes no rejection rule, so any pair of points within the unstated implicit band — which the 28° CONFIRMED gap proves reaches at least that far — passes (wins.json:WIN-028.detail_evidence vague-reading paragraph). The disappearance-claim leg is also independently refuted by NOAA, Lloyd's of London, and the U.S. Coast Guard 1975 review (https://oceanservice.noaa.gov/facts/bermudatri.html), so even a perfect antipodal pair would not become a CONFIRMED prediction without a loss-rate signal.

deeper_analysis:
This is a class:verification queue item — the substantive adversarial work happened at c5/c6/c7, and the c7→c8 decider self-applied the three named patches (H1/H2/H4). My job here is to confirm landing, not to re-attack the WIN. All three patches landed cleanly. The minor stylistic concerns I flag (TLDR "(~5%)" parenthetical, finding 5.3% threshold framing, dropped "tolerance" wording) do not threaten the verdict — they are polish that a defender could attack at the margins but cannot use to invalidate the three-leg refutation. Confidence raised from 0.91 (c7) to 0.93 because the H5/H6/H7 carry-over minors are now also closed via parallel FUSE edits; the WIN is in solid shape with only stylistic polish remaining.

Other-side check: would a smart dome defender accept the current verdict? Probably yes on the loss-rate refutation (NOAA, Lloyd's, USCG are too well-cited to dismiss). On the quantitative miss they would push back hardest — "you're fabricating a 5% threshold we never adopted." The detail responds to this attack adequately by citing the 5.3% as an internal-consistency benchmark only; the TLDR and finding fields are where the attack still has marginal traction (hence holes_found H1 and H2). On the unfalsifiability leg they have less leverage — once they declare a CONFIRMED match at 28°, they have effectively published an implicit lower bound on their acceptance band, and the burden shifts to them to publish an upper bound. Net: the verdict is defensible under aggressive adversarial pressure; the polish items would tighten the margins.

citations_verified: NOAA oceanservice.noaa.gov/facts/bermudatri.html — verified in c5, content unchanged at c8
WMM 2025 / IGRF-13 at ncei.noaa.gov/products/world-magnetic-model — URL now anchored at c8 (was open at c7)
Berlitz 1974 — folklore-zone latitude anchor added at c8
citations_unchecked: CHAOS-7 geomagnetic field model mention — referenced but not linked. Could be hyperlinked to https://www.spacecenter.dk/files/magnetic-models/CHAOS-7/ for future polish, low priority.

summary_for_decider:
PATCH VERIFICATION PASS for queue 335 (c7→c8 H1/H2/H4). All three named patches landed cleanly in 417a887c. As bonus, the c7 carry-over minors H5 (Devil's Sea latitude), H6 (50° vague tolerance), H7 (WMM 2025 URL) were also closed by workspace-sync 7b6eef39 via FUSE operator parallel edits. Verdict Refuted by Data holds; confidence raised 0.91 → 0.93.

Three minor stylistic concerns flagged at c8: (M1) TLDR "(~5%)" parenthetical attributes a numeric threshold to the dome — detail caveats this but TLDR doesn't. (M2) Finding "~3× dome's 5.3% threshold" carries the same issue in the summary table. (M3) TLDR's final sentence drops the "tolerance" gap leg from the c7-H1 recommendation. All three are recommended_action: minor_edit, low priority — polish, not substance. The verdict stands cleanly without these fixes.

WORKFLOW NOTES (not c8 holes, but for decider attention):

(W1) The c7 summary_for_decider recommended closing ISS-1933, 1934, 1935, 1936, 1937, 1948, 1949, 1950, 1951, 1952 as fixed-pending-verification. These 10 issues are STILL OPEN with status assigned-analyst. The c7→c8 decider commit 417a887c only mentions patching H1/H2/H4 and notes "Created no new issues" — it did not close the 10 pre-existing issues. Recommend the next decider run close these 10 issues citing this c8 verification (text is in solid shape, no further work needed).

(W2) DATA INTEGRITY: 9 issues currently appear in BOTH open-issues.json AND closed-issues.json: ISS-1938, 1939, 1940, 1941, 1942, 1943, 1954, 1955, 1956. They show status "assigned-analyst" in open while also being in closed. This is a tracker corruption — likely the decider closed them at one point and they got re-added on a subsequent run (or vice versa). Recommend de-duplication: confirm they are actually closed (text changes addressing them are in place at c8) and remove from open-issues.json. The decider M2 closure-ledger should be cross-checked to identify the source of the re-add.

(W3) c7 summary also recommended consolidating ISS-1938+1941+1954 → single; ISS-1939+1942+1955 → single; ISS-1940+1943+1956 → single (three distinct holes spread across nine open tracker issues). With W2 resolution this becomes moot — once de-duplicated the nine resolve to three genuinely-closed items, no consolidation needed.

Queue 335 status: REVIEW COMPLETE. Pop strict-match. No follow-up cN+1 needed in priority queue. The three c8 minor stylistic holes can route through ordinary M1/M2 work if the decider wants to apply them; none is blocking.
